scan_time_et: Morning recap — US session closed 4:00 PM ET (2026-07-14)

market.advice: The US session closed constructively and broader than it has in weeks. SPY finished 751.86 — above its rising 50-day (741) and only ~0.8% off its high — with the VIX calm at 16.4 and FALLING (below its 16.3 five-day), and breadth a moderate-but-healthy 64% of names above their MA50. The catalysts were friendly: cooler-than-feared inflation data and solid bank earnings lifted the S&P and Nasdaq, cybersecurity names surged (IBM-led), NVDA climbed on positive China headlines, and the US-Iran/Strait-of-Hormuz escalation that hammered semis in the prior session eased (Trump dropped the Hormuz-fee plan in favor of Gulf investment deals). That flips the tape from last session's defensive-only bid toward a genuine broadening: financials — especially the big banks into earnings (GS, MS, BAC all rate TradingView Strong Buy) — cybersecurity/enterprise tech, insurers, industrials, healthcare, utilities and REITs are all working together. For the next session: respect and lean into the uptrend, favor clean, un-extended bases over anything already parabolic (OKTA, CSX, PGNY, SNOW are stretched), and keep an eye on the wave of bank earnings landing over the next 1-3 days as the swing factor. Educational, not financial advice.

picks.thesis: Fortinet is the single strongest setup on the board and it's in the group the tape just rewarded — cybersecurity surged into the close. A tight 6-contraction VCP at a fresh 52-week high, RSI a controlled 66, RS line at the highs on a monster +112% 3-month move. TradingView independently rates it STRONG BUY, so quant, technicals and the second opinion all line up.
strengths: New-high breakout, RSI only 66 — not extended
Cybersecurity is the leadership group; TV STRONG BUY confirms
risks: Earnings in ~16 days — a high-multiple name reacts hard to any billings miss
Guilt-by-association if enterprise-tech spend headlines turn

picks.thesis: Northern Trust broke to a fresh 52-week high out of a tight 3-contraction VCP (tightest 3.6%) with its RS line at the highs — a clean custody/trust leader riding the financials bid that cool inflation and strong bank prints just ignited. TradingView rates it STRONG BUY.
strengths: New high on a tight VCP base, RS at highs
Trust/custody earns more on higher-for-longer rates + rising AUM
risks: Q2 earnings in ~8 days — a two-way catalyst
RSI 71 is warm, though not extended

picks.thesis: Howmet Aerospace is the rare A+ setup that's genuinely un-extended — RSI just 52 and only 2.3% off its high, coiling in a 6-contraction VCP. A best-in-class aerospace-components compounder with structural commercial-aero and defense demand. TradingView agrees at STRONG BUY.
strengths: RSI 52 — lots of runway before overbought
Structural aero/defense demand; TV STRONG BUY
risks: Momentum is steadier (+7% 3-mo) than the high-fliers — this is a base trade, not a chase

picks.thesis: MetLife printed a fresh high out of a 4-contraction VCP with RS at the highs and RSI a reasonable 62 — an insurance leader whose investment book earns more the longer rates stay elevated, and it's in the financial-services group the tape is bidding. TradingView: BUY.
strengths: New high, RS at highs, RSI only 62
Rate tailwind on spread income; TV BUY confirms
risks: A sharp equity pullback pressures AUM/variable-annuity marks

picks.thesis: Dominion Energy made a fresh high out of a tight VCP with RS at the highs and RSI 68 — a regulated utility catching both the defensive bid and the data-center power-demand theme. TradingView independently rates it STRONG BUY.
strengths: New high, RS at highs, tight base
Regulated cash flows + data-center load growth
risks: Rate-sensitive — a back-up in yields would pressure it

picks.thesis: Dell broke to new-high territory (only 1.8% off) out of a 5-contraction VCP with RS at the highs and RSI a cool 57 — remarkable given a +149% 3-month run, so the base has fully digested the move. The AI-server backlog is the engine and TradingView rates it STRONG BUY.
strengths: No earnings for ~52 days; TV STRONG BUY
risks: ISG margins are thinner than pure-play compute
A broad AI-capex scare would hit it hard

picks.thesis: NetApp is basing just 3.3% off its high out of a 3-contraction VCP with RSI 68 after a +77% 3-month run — enterprise storage leveraged to the AI-data-pipeline build-out. TradingView independently rates it STRONG BUY (16 buy / 0 sell).
strengths: Tight base near highs after strong momentum
TV STRONG BUY, zero sell signals

picks.thesis: CVS Health broke to a new high on a powerful +38% 3-month turnaround with RSI a healthy 65 and RS at the highs — a defensive-healthcare re-rating with real momentum. TradingView rates it BUY.
strengths: New high, +38% 3-mo re-rating, RS at highs

picks.thesis: Monster Beverage is right at a 52-week high out of a very tight 4-contraction VCP (tightest 2.6%) with RS at the highs — a defensive-consumer leader with pricing power working while the tape broadens. TradingView: BUY.
strengths: At new highs, tight VCP, RS at highs

picks.thesis: Coca-Cola is basing ~1.4% off its high with RSI a cool 60 — a textbook defensive staple with pricing power and a bulletproof dividend, a steady anchor for a portfolio leaning into more volatile leadership. TradingView: BUY.
W.W. Grainger is basing 1.5% off its high with RSI only 62 and RS at the highs — an industrial-distribution compounder making highs without being extended. TradingView rates it BUY.

First Industrial Realty is basing 0.5% off its high out of a 4-contraction VCP with RSI 62 — an industrial-logistics REIT with durable demand and a rate-relief tailwind if cooling inflation pulls yields lower. TradingView: BUY.
strengths: Tight base right below highs, RSI 62
Benefits from the cool-inflation, lower-yield narrative; TV BUY

picks.thesis: Kimco Realty is basing 2.6% off its high with RSI a cool 51 — an open-air shopping-center REIT that's the least-extended way to play the rate-relief bid, with TradingView independently rating it STRONG BUY.
strengths: RSI 51 — plenty of room to run

picks.thesis: Aflac is basing 1.0% off its high out of a tight 3-contraction VCP with RSI a cool 61 — a steady supplemental-insurance compounder riding the financials bid, un-extended and un-hurried. TradingView: BUY.
strengths: RSI 61 un-extended, tight VCP near highs

picks.thesis: Rubrik is early in its move — 10.6% off its high after a +83% 3-month run, basing in a VCP with RSI 69 — a cyber-resilience/data-security name in the exact group that led the close. TradingView rates it STRONG BUY (17 buy / 0 sell).
strengths: Cybersecurity leadership group; TV STRONG BUY
Early in the base — room before prior highs
risks: 10.6% off high means the base isn't complete; wait for the tell

picks.thesis: EOG Resources is basing 7.2% off its high out of a VCP with RSI 55 — a best-in-class shale operator and the portfolio's energy hedge if Middle East supply headlines flare again. TradingView: BUY.
strengths: Low-cost shale leader, strong FCF + dividend
Energy hedge against geopolitical oil spikes
risks: Direct oil-price exposure — cuts both ways

picks.thesis: Corteva is basing 2.1% off its high out of a 4-contraction VCP with RSI 68 — an ag-inputs leader (seed + crop protection) that diversifies the book away from tech and financials. TradingView: BUY.

Entergy is basing 1.7% off its high out of a VCP with RSI a cool 56 — a Gulf-South regulated utility with heavy industrial/data-center load growth. TradingView independently rates it STRONG BUY (16 buy / 0 sell).
strengths: TV STRONG BUY, zero sell signals
risks: Storm/regulatory risk in its service territory; earnings ~15 days out

picks.thesis: STAG Industrial is basing right at its high (0.3% off) out of a VCP with RSI 61 — an industrial-REIT compounder with a monthly dividend and a rate-relief tailwind. TradingView rates it STRONG BUY (16 buy / 0 sell).

Goldman Sachs is at its 52-week high with RS at the highs and TradingView rates it STRONG BUY (17 buy / 0 sell) — but earnings are essentially now (0 days), so this is a WATCH into the print, not a fresh entry. Strong bank prints already lifted the group; let GS report before adding.
strengths: At new highs, RS at highs; TV STRONG BUY
Trading/IB tailwind from the risk-on tape
risks: EARNINGS IMMINENT (~0 days) — event risk; don't chase into it
A sell-the-news reaction after a big run is common

picks.thesis: Morgan Stanley is at its high with RS at the highs and TradingView rates it STRONG BUY (17 buy / 0 sell), RSI a cool 52 — a clean setup fundamentally, but earnings land in ~1 day, so watch the reaction rather than entering ahead of it.
strengths: At highs, RSI 52 un-extended; TV STRONG BUY

picks.thesis: Travelers is basing 2.0% off its high out of a tight 3-contraction VCP with RS at the highs — an A+ P&C insurer, but earnings are ~3 days out, so treat it as a WATCH into the report. TradingView: BUY.
strengths: Tight VCP near highs, RS at highs
Hard-market P&C pricing power; TV BUY

picks.thesis: Elevance Health is at its high with RS at the highs on a +38% 3-month move and TradingView rates it STRONG BUY — but earnings are ~1 day out, so it's a WATCH. A managed-care leader that would confirm the healthcare-leadership theme on a clean print.
strengths: At highs, RS at highs; TV STRONG BUY
risks: EARNINGS in ~1 day — medical-cost-ratio prints move this hard
Utilization surprises are the perennial risk

picks.thesis: Okta is a cybersecurity leader at new highs and TradingView rates it STRONG BUY — but RSI is 80 after a +141% 3-month vertical run, so it's extended. Right group, wrong entry: watch for a pullback or a tight flag rather than chasing.
strengths: Cyber leadership group; TV STRONG BUY (17 buy / 0 sell)
risks: RSI 80 — extended; chasing invites a sharp shakeout
High multiple; wait for a proper base

picks.thesis: CSX is at a new high with RS at the highs and TradingView rates it BUY — a rail bellwether confirming the industrial/transport bid — but RSI 83 is very extended, so watch for a rest before entering.
strengths: New high, RS at highs; TV BUY
risks: RSI 83 — overbought; entry risk is timing

picks.thesis: NVIDIA climbed into the close on positive China headlines and sits 10% off its high in a VCP with RSI 61 — the AI bellwether stabilizing as the semi complex recovers. TradingView rates it BUY (16 buy / 1 sell). Watch for the base to complete and reclaim of prior highs.
strengths: RSI 61, basing rather than extended; TV BUY
risks: 10% off high — base not yet complete
China-export-policy headline whipsaw cuts both ways

picks.thesis: AMD is only 5.6% off its high after a +115% 3-month run with RSI 55 — the closest of the big semis to reclaiming highs as the group recovers. TradingView rates it BUY. Watch for a new-high breakout to trigger.
risks: High-beta — moves hard with the semi tape

picks.thesis: Eli Lilly is basing 6.6% off its high with RSI 57 — the premier GLP-1 secular grower (Zepbound/Mounjaro) — but here the second opinion diverges: TradingView rates it only NEUTRAL (8 buy / 8 sell), reflecting the still-incomplete base. Fundamentally a core holding; technically wait for the base to firm.
strengths: Incretin franchise still scaling — best secular grower in healthcare
risks: TV NEUTRAL — technicals not yet confirming the fundamentals
Very high share price — size carefully
GLP-1 competitive/pricing headlines; earnings ~22 days out

picks.thesis: NextEra Energy is early — 7.9% off its high in a VCP with RSI 63 — the largest regulated + renewables utility, a rate-relief and power-demand beneficiary. TradingView: BUY. Watch for the base to tighten and clear resistance.
strengths: Rate-relief tailwind if yields ease; TV BUY
risks: 7.9% off high — earlier in its base than the utility leaders
